releases.title: collectZ v3.22.0
releases.summary: This release adds a Comic-Con field-test workflow inside Events so an event can become a mobile hunt list, quick haul capture surface, and post-con cleanup queue. The slice is additive and keeps collectZ on the `3.x` line because it builds on existing Events, Wishlist, Art, Collectibles, purchased-item, and companion snapshot contracts.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added nullable `booth` support to wishlist items.
Added `event_id` filtering to the Wishlist list API.
Added `GET /api/events/:id/field-kit` for event-scoped hunt items, tracked purchases, cleanup gaps, and a secret-free companion snapshot summary.
Added a Comic-Con field kit panel to the Event drawer.
Refined the Event drawer into Overview, Hunt, Haul, Plans, Purchases, and Cleanup tabs so the Comic-Con field workflow is available without turning the drawer into one long scroll.
Flattened the Hunt, Plans, and Purchases tabs into divider-led rows and inline readbacks so event workflow sections match the rest of the drawer surface.
Added Con Hunt actions for event-linked wishlist items: mark ordered, acquired, or dismissed; edit vendor, booth, and notes; launch quick haul capture.
Added Quick Haul Capture for event-linked Art and Collectibles, including purchased-item linking and optional wishlist acquisition.
Added Post-Con Cleanup readback for linked purchases missing vendor, booth, price, or photo.
Hardened the backend container startup path so reused local upload volumes are writable by the non-root Node process.

releases.title: collectZ v3.21.0
releases.summary: This release makes collectZ dashboard destinations first-class browser routes instead of representing every app view as `/dashboard?tab=...`. Library, workspace, profile, help, and platform surfaces now have canonical paths that match their navigation meaning, while existing dashboard query links remain compatible.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a central canonical route map for dashboard destinations.
Added platform routes such as `/platform/settings`, `/platform/runtime`, `/platform/runtime/logs`, `/platform/users`, and `/platform/workspaces`.
Added workspace routes such as `/workspace/settings` and `/workspace/review`.
Kept library routes under `/library/...`, including `/library/movies`, `/library/wishlist`, `/library/capture`, and `/library/import`.
Updated app-shell history handling so navigation pushes first-class routes and legacy query URLs are replaced with the canonical path.
Preserved compatibility for older `/dashboard?tab=...` and `/admin/integrations/...` links.
Removed the remaining Core-hosted platform support UI, frontend support-session plumbing, support-session banner, and Help Admin copy so Core Help now contains only guidance and releases.

Removed the unreachable Core platform integration diagnostic handlers for PriceCharting, eBay, and structured-log tests while keeping explicit Core-boundary 404 responses for those `cairn`-owned routes.
Removed dormant platform email-delivery and analytics settings controls from the Core settings view.
Removed the unmounted platform API docs and metrics route modules from Core after those surfaces moved to `cairn`.
Removed the obsolete admin-space control smoke and updated Core support/tenancy smokes so fixture workspaces are created directly while `cairn`-owned admin workspace routes remain blocked.

Removed stale workspace-manager browser shell coverage tied to moved global admin workspace/user routes, while keeping scoped workspace behavior covered by backend/unit smokes and preserving library lifecycle browser coverage with direct fixture membership setup.

releases.title: collectZ v3.20.10
releases.summary: This patch adds a Cairn-owned platform analytics setting for Rybbit and loads the configured tracking script globally in the collectZ browser app. Platform operators can save the Rybbit script URL, site id, and optional `data-*` attributes from Platform Settings without storing arbitrary inline JavaScript.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added Cairn analytics tracking settings under `/api/admin/settings/analytics`.
Added public Cairn browser config at `/api/platform/analytics`.
Added Platform Settings controls for enabling analytics, setting the script URL, site id, and optional data attributes.
Added a collectZ root-level analytics loader so enabled tracking is applied across auth, dashboard, and standalone app routes.
Updated Cairn OpenAPI docs and helper coverage for the new settings contract.

releases.title: collectZ v3.20.9
releases.summary: This patch moves Plex reconciliation conflict review match-reason filtering into the backend query. The Plex Sync conflict review dropdown now reflects server-provided reason counts, and selected match reasons are applied before the endpoint row limit instead of filtering only the rows already loaded in the browser.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `matchedBy` filtering to the Plex reconciliation conflict-review endpoint.
Returned match-reason counts for the selected open or resolved conflict-review status.
Updated the Plex Sync conflict review dropdown to use backend-provided reason counts.
Reloaded conflict review rows when the selected match reason changes.
Added source coverage for the backend filter contract and frontend filter wiring.
Added a local `ci:prepare` script that mirrors the GitHub prepare gate checks.

releases.title: collectZ v3.20.8
releases.summary: Persisted Plex readback refresh cadence in the Plex Sync settings.

releases.title: collectZ v3.20.5
releases.summary: This patch adds Plex reconciliation review filters. The Plex Sync tab can switch between open and resolved conflicts, filter loaded reviews by match reason, and read resolved review outcomes without reopening them.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added Open and Resolved filters to Plex conflict review.
Added match-reason filtering for loaded Plex conflict rows.
Shows filtered and total conflict counts.
Displays resolved conflict outcomes without action buttons.
Kept existing conflict resolution behavior unchanged.

releases.title: collectZ v3.20.4
releases.summary: This patch makes Plex activity entries easier to read. Workspace activity now recognizes Plex imports, webhook queue events, webhook processor runs, and webhook import outcomes instead of showing generic provider activity.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added explicit Plex import summaries to the activity feed.
Added readable Plex webhook queued and processed activity summaries.
Added readable Plex webhook auto-processor activity summaries.
Added Plex job, rating key, and section readback to activity snapshots.
Kept existing Plex import, sync, webhook, readback, and writeback behavior unchanged.

releases.title: collectZ v3.20.3
releases.summary: This patch makes Plex initial import a deliberate Sync workflow. The Plex Sync tab now shows selected libraries and media types before queuing import work, and the old global Plex import button has been removed.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a compact `Initial import` block to Settings > Integrations > Plex > Sync.
Shows selected Plex libraries from Setup before starting an import.
Shows inferred media types for selected Plex sections when detected library metadata is available.
Warns about saved section IDs that are not currently detected.
Prevents queuing a Plex import when no library sections are selected.
Removed the duplicate bottom-level `Import from Plex` action from the integration page.
